_comment: Pterodactyl-compatible export of the Palworld (Official Docker Image) egg. Importable into Pelican and Pterodactyl.
name: Palworld (Official Docker Image)
author: pascal.hannemann@gmail.com
description:
Palworld dedicated server installed from the OFFICIAL Docker image published by Pocketpair
(ghcr.io/pocketpairjp/palserver). The install script fetches the official image's layers from
the GHCR registry and extracts the server files into the server volume; to update the server,
simply use the panel's "Reinstall" button - it fetches the official image again (version
selectable via the Image Tag variable, default "latest") while preserving your world data
(Pal/Saved). Server settings are managed through panel variables and
Pal/Saved/Config/LinuxServer/PalWorldSettings.ini.
startup: ([ -x /opt/paltools/watcher.sh ] && /opt/paltools/watcher.sh > /dev/null 2>&1 &); ./PalworldServerConfigParser; (while read cmd; do rcon -s -a "localhost:$RCON_PORT" -p "$ADMIN_PASSWORD" "$cmd";done) < /dev/stdin & /home/container/Pal/Binaries/Linux/PalServer-Linux-Shipping Pal $(if [ "${COMMUNITY_SERVER}" == "1" ]; then echo "-publiclobby"; fi) $(if [ "${PERF_THREADS}" == "1" ]; then echo "-useperfthreads -NoAsyncLoadingThread -UseMultithreadForDS"; fi) -port={{SERVER_PORT}} -publicport={{SERVER_PORT}} -servername="{{SERVER_NAME}}" -players={{MAX_PLAYERS}} $(if [ -n "$SERVER_PASSWORD" ]; then echo "-serverpassword=\"${SERVER_PASSWORD}\""; fi) -adminpassword="{{ADMIN_PASSWORD}}" -rcon

installation.script:
#!/bin/bash
# Palworld installation from the OFFICIAL dedicated server Docker image
# (ghcr.io/pocketpairjp/palserver) - WITHOUT running that image directly:
# it declares ENTRYPOINT ["/bin/sh","/pal/Package/PalServer.sh"] and Wings
# passes the install entrypoint as docker Cmd, so using it as the install
# container would boot the game instead of this script. Instead, the
# image's layers are pulled straight from the GHCR registry and the
# server files (/pal/Package) are extracted from them.
#
# Install container: ghcr.io/parkervcp/installers:debian (curl/jq/tar included)
# Server Files:      /mnt/server
#
# UPDATING: use the panel's "Reinstall" button. It fetches the official
# image again (tag from the IMAGE TAG variable, default "latest") while
# preserving your world data and config (Pal/Saved).

set -euo pipefail

REGISTRY=ghcr.io
REPO=pocketpairjp/palserver
TAG="${OFFICIAL_IMAGE_TAG:-latest}"
ACCEPT='application/vnd.oci.image.index.v1+json,application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.list.v2+json,application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json,application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v2+json'

echo "Requesting anonymous pull token for ${REPO} ..."
TOKEN=$(curl -fsSL "https://${REGISTRY}/token?scope=repository:${REPO}:pull" | jq -r .token)

echo "Resolving manifest for tag '${TAG}' ..."
MANIFEST=$(curl -fsSL -H "Authorization: Bearer ${TOKEN}" -H "Accept: ${ACCEPT}" "https://${REGISTRY}/v2/${REPO}/manifests/${TAG}")

# Multi-arch index -> pick the linux/amd64 manifest
if jq -e '.manifests' > /dev/null <<< "${MANIFEST}"; then
    DIGEST=$(jq -r '[.manifests[] | select(.platform.os == "linux" and .platform.architecture == "amd64")][0].digest' <<< "${MANIFEST}")
    MANIFEST=$(curl -fsSL -H "Authorization: Bearer ${TOKEN}" -H "Accept: ${ACCEPT}" "https://${REGISTRY}/v2/${REPO}/manifests/${DIGEST}")
fi

LAYERS=$(jq -r '.layers[].digest' <<< "${MANIFEST}")
TOTAL_MB=$(jq -r '[.layers[].size] | add / 1048576 | floor' <<< "${MANIFEST}")
echo "Image tag '${TAG}': $(wc -l <<< "${LAYERS}") layers, ~${TOTAL_MB} MB to download."

# Preserve world data + config across reinstalls (kept on the same mount,
# /tmp is a small tmpfs and saves can be large).
if [ -d /mnt/server/Pal/Saved ]; then
    echo "Existing save data found - preserving Pal/Saved ..."
    rm -rf /mnt/server/.Saved_keep
    mv /mnt/server/Pal/Saved /mnt/server/.Saved_keep
fi

# Extract /pal/Package from every layer, in order (later layers win).
# Base OS layers simply contain no server files and are skipped.
BLOB=/mnt/server/.layer.tmp
for digest in ${LAYERS}; do
    LAYER_MB=$(jq -r --arg d "${digest}" '[.layers[] | select(.digest == $d).size][0] / 1048576 | floor' <<< "${MANIFEST}")
    echo "Downloading layer ${digest} (${LAYER_MB} MB) ..."
    rm -f "${BLOB}"
    curl -fsSL --http1.1 --retry 5 --retry-all-errors --retry-delay 3 -C - -o "${BLOB}" -H "Authorization: Bearer ${TOKEN}" "https://${REGISTRY}/v2/${REPO}/blobs/${digest}" &
    CURL_PID=$!
    while kill -0 "${CURL_PID}" 2> /dev/null; do
        sleep 2
        if [ -f "${BLOB}" ] && kill -0 "${CURL_PID}" 2> /dev/null; then
            echo "  ... $(du -m "${BLOB}" | cut -f1) / ${LAYER_MB} MB downloaded"
        fi
    done
    wait "${CURL_PID}" || { echo "ERROR: download of layer ${digest} failed."; exit 1; }
    echo "  download finished, extracting (the game data layer takes a few minutes) ..."
    tar -xzf "${BLOB}" -C /mnt/server --strip-components=2 --wildcards 'pal/Package/*' 2> /dev/null \
        && echo "  -> server files extracted" \
        || echo "  -> no server files in this layer, skipped"
    rm -f "${BLOB}"
done

if [ ! -f /mnt/server/Pal/Binaries/Linux/PalServer-Linux-Shipping ]; then
    echo "ERROR: PalServer-Linux-Shipping missing after extraction - installation failed."
    exit 1
fi

if [ -d /mnt/server/.Saved_keep ]; then
    rm -rf /mnt/server/Pal/Saved
    mv /mnt/server/.Saved_keep /mnt/server/Pal/Saved
fi

# Steamworks SDK library where the server binary looks for it (HOME=/home/container at runtime)
mkdir -p /mnt/server/.steam/sdk64
cp -f /mnt/server/linux64/steamclient.so /mnt/server/.steam/sdk64/steamclient.so

# Seed the config file on first install
if [ ! -f /mnt/server/Pal/Saved/Config/LinuxServer/PalWorldSettings.ini ]; then
    echo "Creating PalWorldSettings.ini from defaults ..."
    mkdir -p /mnt/server/Pal/Saved/Config/LinuxServer
    if [ -f /mnt/server/DefaultPalWorldSettings.ini ]; then
        cp /mnt/server/DefaultPalWorldSettings.ini /mnt/server/Pal/Saved/Config/LinuxServer/PalWorldSettings.ini
    else
        printf '[/Script/Pal.PalGameWorldSettings]\nOptionSettings=()\n' > /mnt/server/Pal/Saved/Config/LinuxServer/PalWorldSettings.ini
    fi
fi

# Config parser applies panel variables (RCON, description, crossplay, ...)
# to PalWorldSettings.ini on every start.
cd /mnt/server
echo "Downloading PalworldServerConfigParser ..."
curl -sSL -o PalworldServerConfigParser https://github.com/pelican-eggs/Palworld-Config-Parser-Tool/releases/latest/download/PalworldServerConfigParser-linux-amd64
chmod +x PalworldServerConfigParser

chmod +x /mnt/server/PalServer.sh /mnt/server/Pal/Binaries/Linux/PalServer-Linux-Shipping 2>/dev/null || true

echo "-----------------------------------------"
echo "Installation completed (server files from the official image)"
echo "To update later: stop the server and press 'Reinstall' in the panel."
echo "-----------------------------------------"

variables.name: Server Name
variables.description: The name of the server shown in the server browser. The characters " ( ) are not allowed - they corrupt the config parser.

variables.name: Server Description
variables.description: The description of the server. The characters " ( ) are not allowed - they corrupt the config parser.

variables.name: Max Players
variables.description: Maximum number of players allowed on the server (1-32).

variables.name: Server Password
variables.description: If specified, players must provide this password to join the server.

variables.name: Admin Password
variables.description:
Password for administrator commands (in-game chat and RCON). Required - it is also used by
the panel console and the stop command, which are forwarded to the server via RCON.

variables.name: Community Server
variables.description: Set to 1 to register the server in the community server browser (-publiclobby). Leave at 0 for a private/invite (IP + port) server.

variables.name: Public IP
variables.description: Set this to the server's public IP address. Only needed if the allocation is a local IP, otherwise the allocated IP is used automatically.

variables.name: Crossplay Platforms
variables.description:
Comma-separated list of platforms allowed to connect. Valid values: Steam, Xbox, PS5, Mac.
Example: Steam,Xbox,PS5,Mac

variables.name: RCON Port
variables.description: Internal RCON port used by the panel console. Does not need to be allocated.

variables.name: Enable RCON
variables.description: Must be True - the panel console and stop command depend on it.

variables.name: Performance Threading Flags
variables.description:
Set to 1 to add the legacy -useperfthreads -NoAsyncLoadingThread -UseMultithreadForDS flags.
The official docs state these may be unnecessary or even harmful on v1.0+ — leave at 0 unless
testing shows they help on your hardware.

variables.name: Image Tag (Server Version)
variables.description:
Tag of the official Docker image (ghcr.io/pocketpairjp/palserver) to install from, e.g.
"latest" or "v1.0.1.100619". Applied when the server is (re)installed.

variables.name: Auto Update (SteamCMD)
variables.description: Keep at 0. This egg updates through the official Docker image via the Reinstall button, not SteamCMD.

variables.name: Enable REST API
variables.description: Enables the official HTTP REST API (players, kick/ban, metrics, announce) used by the panel's Palworld Admin plugin. Applied to PalWorldSettings.ini by the config parser on every start. The REST API port must be allocated to the server and should be firewalled to the panel host only - it must NEVER be reachable from the public internet.

variables.name: REST API Port
variables.description: Port for the official REST API. Needs a matching allocation on the server (TCP). Used by the Palworld Admin panel plugin.
